title: What does my body do when the number is good?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining the somatic response to financial abundance.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for noticing what the body does when money is good: the tightening, the scan, the waiting, and what a different response might feel like.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. The prompts start with physical observation rather than belief analysis, the body's response is often the first clue.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
When I open my bank account and the number is good, what does my body do first?
Write the physical response. Where do you feel it? Does it tighten, soften, scan?
What thought or image follows that physical response?
Write it plainly. Is it a threat, a doubt, a prediction?
Where did I learn that abundance was something to brace against rather than settle into?
Write the source if it comes: a person, an event, a repeated experience. If nothing specific comes, write "I don't know" and keep going.
What would my body do differently if I fully believed the overflow was safe and staying?
Write the physical version: where the tension releases, what the breath does, how the face settles.
What is one small thing I can do today to practise the body response I want to have when money is good?
Write a specific action. Checking the account and choosing to relax. Saying the number out loud. Pausing before you close the app.
Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
